name: Los Angeles Unified School District
districtFact: The second-largest school district in the United States, serving approximately 420,000 students across 32 cities and unincorporated county areas throughout the Los Angeles basin.

about.title: About Los Angeles Unified School District
about.content: LAUSD is one of the most structurally complex school districts in the country. Beyond traditional neighborhood schools, it includes more than 60 magnet schools, a network of Dual Language Immersion programs spanning Spanish, Korean, Armenian, and other languages, the Transitional Kindergarten (TK) program operating across hundreds of campuses, and a Local Districts system that divides the district into semi-autonomous geographic zones. This page reflects the district-wide instructional calendar. Individual schools, affiliated charter schools, and specialized programs often follow different schedules. Confirm your school's specific calendar at lausd.net before booking travel or arranging childcare around these dates.

about.title: What New LAUSD Families Should Know
about.content: LAUSD's scale creates real variation between schools. A family in Silver Lake and a family in Chatsworth may share the same district-wide calendar but experience very different school rhythms — different minimum day schedules, different after-school offerings, different parent community cultures. The district-wide calendar on this page establishes the dates every traditional-calendar school shares: the same instructional start, major breaks, and state holidays. Everything else — early dismissal days, site-level professional development, school events — is set at the individual school level. The most effective thing a new LAUSD family can do is request the site-level calendar from your school's main office in August, before the year begins.

planningTips.title: Things LAUSD Families Often Miss
content: Not all LAUSD schools follow the Traditional Calendar shown here. Some campuses operate on year-round or alternative schedules with different start dates and breaks. Confirm which calendar your school publishes at lausd.net before booking travel or arranging childcare around these dates.

Cesar Chavez Day / Farm Workers Day (March 31) is a California state school holiday. Parents relocating from other states often miss it — in years when it falls outside Spring Recess, it's a standalone no-school day that requires separate childcare planning.

Individual LAUSD schools schedule their own minimum days (Minimum Mondays, early-release days) throughout the year. Contact your school's office in early September to get a site-level list of those dates.
LAUSD Spring Recess falls in late March — earlier than many Southern and Midwestern districts. If coordinating with out-of-state family, confirm that their break aligns before booking travel.

districtFaqs.q: Why do some LAUSD schools have different calendars?
districtFaqs.a: This page reflects LAUSD's district-wide instructional calendar. Individual schools, charter schools, and special programs may follow different schedules. The most reliable way to confirm your school's calendar is to check the school-specific schedule at lausd.net — the district-wide calendar shown here may not apply to every campus.

districtFaqs.q: Why is Farm Workers Day a school holiday in LAUSD?
districtFaqs.a: Farm Workers Day (March 31) is associated with Cesar Chavez and the farm labor movement. It doesn't appear on most school calendars outside California, so families relocating from other states often miss it. In years when it falls outside Spring Recess, it creates a standalone no-school day that requires separate childcare planning.

districtFaqs.q: What are minimum days at LAUSD?
districtFaqs.a: Minimum days (sometimes called Minimum Mondays) are days when students are dismissed significantly earlier than usual — often 2 to 3 hours earlier. LAUSD uses minimum days for teacher professional development, parent-teacher conferences, and school-specific planning. These are not shown on the district-wide calendar; contact your school's main office for a site-level minimum day schedule.

districtFaqs.q: Are charter schools included in the LAUSD calendar?
districtFaqs.a: No. Independent charter schools that operate within the LAUSD boundary set their own academic calendars and are not required to follow the LAUSD district calendar. Some affiliated charters may align closely, but many differ in start dates, break schedules, and instructional days. Verify directly with your charter school.

livingHere.intro: Los Angeles Unified stretches across 710 square miles — from the San Fernando Valley to the South Bay, from Hollywood to East LA. For families relocating to the region, a few local realities intersect directly with the school calendar.

highlights.label: Los Angeles International Airport (LAX)
highlights.detail: One of the world's busiest airports. During LAUSD Spring Recess (late March) and the week school ends in early June, departing flights and parking fill quickly — booking 6–8 weeks ahead is standard for popular routes during those windows.

highlights.label: Traffic and Commute Patterns
highlights.detail: LA's freeway system is among the most congested in the US. Morning school drop-off aligns with peak commute hours on the 405, 101, and 10. On minimum days — when students dismiss 2–3 hours early — afternoon traffic patterns shift unexpectedly, which catches new residents off guard in the first semester.

highlights.label: Wildfire and Air Quality Season
highlights.detail: Late summer through early fall (August–November) is peak fire season in Southern California. Air quality alerts can suspend school outdoor activities or trigger campus lockdowns. LAUSD has emergency protocols for severe air quality events. Families with students who have respiratory conditions should keep an eye on AQI forecasts during this period.

highlights.label: Entertainment Industry Calendar
highlights.detail: A significant portion of LAUSD families work in film, TV, and related industries. Production schedules — including pilot season (January–April) and awards season — can affect parent availability in ways that don't follow a standard corporate calendar. School pick-up coverage and childcare arrangements here often need more flexibility than in other metros.

highlights.label: Earthquake Preparedness
highlights.detail: LAUSD schools conduct mandatory earthquake drills in the first weeks of school each fall. Families new to California should register for ShakeAlertLA and review LAUSD's emergency reunification procedures before the school year begins — the reunification process requires a parent or authorized adult to sign out students in person.

relatedDistricts.name: Long Beach Unified School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Long Beach Unified is one of the larger districts adjacent to LAUSD; Spring Break in LBUSD falls in early April while LAUSD's Spring Recess runs in late March — the two breaks don't align, which matters for households with children in both districts.

relatedDistricts.name: Burbank Unified School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Burbank Unified serves Burbank, directly adjacent to the San Fernando Valley portion of LAUSD. Families near the Burbank–North Hollywood boundary should confirm which district their address falls in.

relatedDistricts.name: Glendale Unified School District
relatedDistricts.reason: Glendale Unified is adjacent to the northeast portion of LAUSD. The two districts share California state holiday observances but may differ in start dates and Spring Recess timing.
